comments.text: The aerial view of Vietnam in this video is exactly what I see every time I fly into the country. comments.author: @tonyvu60
comments.text: I love coming back to Vietnam and experience the language, the culture, the people, and the family ties that makes Vietnam unique. BUT that was only because I have money to spend. Without the US dollar, I’d be just another soul drifting in a noisy and fast pace life in the city. And rather boring in the rural area. A stark reminder, especially to me, is the food. Yes, it is diverse and a lot of foods are unheard of, never seen before. But the quality is nowhere compared to the Vietnamese food here in the states and that is the fact. Sorry to rain on the parade and tell it like it is.

replies.author: @TraceTheGlobe
replies.text: Thank you for sharing your honest perspective. Every person experiences a country differently, shaped by their own background and expectations. We're glad Vietnam keeps drawing you back—the language, culture, people, and family ties you mentioned are exactly what make it special for so many. Thank you for adding your thoughts to the conversation.

comments.author: @CongBaNguyen
comments.text:
Vietnam is a country where the land and its people are inseparable. For thousands of years, our ancestors and our nation fought to preserve and reclaim our homeland from powerful empires, shaping a resilient and proud national identity. 
Today, Vietnam is one of the few countries that maintains friendly relations and cooperation with nations across different political and cultural spheres, including the United States, Russia, China, North Korea, South Korea, Japan, Iran, Israel, France, Germany, and many countries throughout the European Union.
Vietnam is also known for its long-standing culture, rich history, and diverse cuisine that is both delicious and affordable. Above all, the Vietnamese people are friendly, compassionate, open-hearted, and welcoming to people of all races, appearances, and backgrounds.
Vietnam is widely recognized as a peaceful and stable country, where people can live, travel, and connect with a strong sense of security and community. Beyond its natural beauty and cultural heritage, Vietnam offers something deeper: visitors often discover a spirit of kindness and humanity that can awaken compassion and empathy within every person who experiences the country and its people.

replies.author: @TraceTheGlobe
replies.text: Beautifully expressed. Vietnam’s strength lies not only in its history and achievements, but also in the character and generosity of its people. Thank you for sharing your perspective. ❤

comments.author: @NomadEchoes-f9f
comments.text: Vietnam is absolutely stunning, but no trip is complete without trying a fresh bánh mì from a street vendor and a steaming bowl of phở. The landscapes are unforgettable, but the food is what will keep calling you back.

replies.author: @TraceTheGlobe
replies.text: We couldn't agree more. A great bánh mì and a bowl of phở are unforgettable parts of the Vietnam experience.

comments.author: @SacredVietnamForest
comments.text: "Vietnam is not only breathtakingly beautiful but also deeply sacred, where a rich tapestry of spiritual and cultural traditions touches the soul."

comments.text: I'd like to add some information: Ha Long Bay is indeed a tourist destination, but if you only talk about the fishermen there, viewers might mistakenly think the area is poor and only focuses on fishing. comments.author: @chaymeeable
comments.text:
As a Filipino, with the same history of colonization, I regard Vietnam highly. 🫡

I agree that Vietnam's greatest wealth is the people. ❤ I just hope that Phu Quoc will not be like Phuket or Bali as I believe that this marvelous island should remain free from the toxic effects of over tourism.

I'm so glad to spent my birthday last year in Da Nang and Hoi an. And I can't wait to be back again to experience Ninh Binh, Sapa, and Hanoi. 🎉

Thank you for this amazing portrayal of Vietnam! ❤❤❤

replies.author: @TraceTheGlobe
replies.text: Thank you so much for your thoughtful comment. We're delighted to hear that you enjoyed your time in Da Nang and Hoi An, and we share your hope that places like Phu Quoc can preserve their natural beauty and character. Wishing you many more unforgettable journeys in Vietnam—Ninh Binh, Sapa, and Hanoi will be waiting for you!

comments.text: This isn't just a documentary; it’s an emotional journey. I was especially impressed by the segment on coffee culture and the incredible hospitality of the Vietnamese people. Definitely a bucket-list destination!
